[PDF_PAGE 202 | score=20] Quest Spells f he maintained by granting quest spells only in true catastrophes. Excepfional and unique circumstances will arise that will draw quest magic info the game. This may include racial interests (for elves, dwarves, and so on) such as defense of the homelands or protection of great fortresses, or it may include communities of exceptional artisans wishing to draw quest magic from Powers. The discovery of an intensely magi- cal artifact or place important to the power may necessitate quest magic to secure it. Establishing and developing a major sacred location may justify the use ofquest magic (especially with spheres such as Creation, Guardian, Protection, and Wards). Such cases are
[PDF_PAGE 73 | score=15] Tongues (Alteration) Reversible Sphere: Divination Level: 4 Range: 0 Components: Y S Casting Time: 7 Duration: 1 tum Area of Effect: The caster Saving Throw: None This spell enables the caster to speak and under- stand additional languages, whether they are racial tongues or regional dialects, but not communica- tions of animals or mindless creatures. When the spell is cast, the spellcaster selects the language or languages to be understood. The spell then empow- ers the caster with the ability to speak and under- stand the language desired with perfect fluency and accent. The spell enables the priest to he understood by all speakers of that language within hearing dis- tance, usually 60 fee
[PDF_PAGE 84 | score=15] Tree Steed Tree Spirit (Necromancy) Sphere: Plant Level: 7 Range: Touch Components: Y S CastingTime: 1 turn Duration: Instantaneous Area of Effect: 1 tree Saving Throw: None Tree spirif permanently links the soul of the caster with a tree, usually chosen carefully for its health, vigor, and remote environment. Casting this spell joins the life force of the druid with that of the tree; as long as the tree lives, the caster ages atone- tenth the normal rate. (Because the spell causes the tree to devote all its energy to maintaining health rather than growth, it always remains exactly the size it was at the time of casting.) Moreover, the caster’s spirit merges with the tree at the charac- ter’
[PDF_PAGE 169 | score=15] Tattoos of Protection (EnchantmentJCharm) Sphere: Protection Level: Quest Range: Touch Components: V, S, M Duration: 1 mo. Casting Time: 1 hdtattoo Area of Effect: 1 creature Saving Throw: None When the Prince ofTerror’s legions enter battle, he sometimes grants his war priests the ability to protect noble followers. By use of this spell, a priest of Belinik can inscribe protective tattoos onto one creature (which may he the caster). A tattoo ofpmtection can contain the magic of any priest spell from the sphere of protection that the priest could normally cast. The priest can inscribe as one level ofmagic into such tattoos for each level of experience. A 12th-level priest of Belinik, for exa

When cast by a priest of 1 lth level or higher, .thaumaturgy has an additional effect. Ifthe suh- ! is within the normal range of the subsequent 11 (for example, 80 yards for charm person), the ject's saving throw suffers a -2 penalty Like the personal reading spell, telethau- turgy functions only if the priest knows the rect name of the subject. A priest who casts spell using an alias will not know that ?thaumaturgy has not taken effect until the #sequent spell fails. The priest does not auto- tically know why the

It cannot he tuned, affected or dispelled hy holy beings or things. The material components of this spell are a pinch of hone powder or a bone shard, a pinch of dust, and a fairly complete, nonmagical, non- aligned suit of metal armor, which serves a$ the body of the helmed horror. [PDF_PAGE 187 | score=14] The insect host can he dissipated by a success- ful dispel magic. It can also he blown back by a powerful, magical wind dispersed for a time by an air elemental, or temporarily held off with smoke, thought the last will only work for a few rounds. Area-effect magic such asfirehall can destroy large numbers of the magical insects, hut individual attacks have very little effect. The material component is a blown glass model of a locust or some other insect, filled with gem dust (I 00 gp minimum). Notes: Granted to air elementals, common. [PDF_PAGE 52 | score=13] Susrarre, Chariot of Suspended Animation (Necromancy) Sphere: Necromantic Level: 4 Range: Touch Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 7 Duration: Special Area of Effect: Creature touched Saving Throw: None By using this spell, the caster can place one will- ing subject in a state ofsuspended animation. The victim’s breathing, heartbeat, and other vital processes slow to the point of nonexistence, although he or she seems to be deeply asleep, not dead. A caster of 7th to loth level can maintain the suspended animation for up to one week plus one day per level; a caster of 1 Ith to 15th level can maintain the state for up io one month plus one week per level; and a caster of 16th level or higher c
